title: Local-rival undercut opportunity features instead of only global race context
summary: F1 strategy literature emphasizes undercut/overcut and opponent interaction, not just standalone tyre life.
rationale: F1 strategy literature emphasizes undercut/overcut and opponent interaction, not just standalone tyre life. Your current best features are mostly self-state plus coarse race medians; adjacent gains may come from encoding whether a stop right now creates a strategic advantage versus nearby cars.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race-Year-LapNumber`, build features against drivers in positions `-2..+2`: relative tyre life, relative lap-time delta, relative degradation, whether neighbors are on different compounds, and whether the driver is older-slower than cars immediately ahead/behind. Add interaction flags for late-race plus different-compound plus worn-tyre cases.
expected_effect: Should help identify pit waves and strategic reaction stops that depend on nearby competitors rather than absolute wear alone.
risk: Without gap-to-car timings, position-only proxies are crude; noisy rival features may help only in certain race phases and require a specialist model or strong regularization.
